name: Chili "Con-causasian" (White Chili)
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Chicken Breast; skinned, boned, diced
1 18 ounce can Tomatillos, drained and coarsely chopped
1 can Chopped Green Chile Peppers, not drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Spray a large skillet with Cooking Oil Spray (Pam) and heat over medium high.
2. Add Olive Oil to the heated pan and let it warm up.
3. Add diced chicken to the pan and sauté until cooked through, about 3 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
4. Add shallots and garlic to the pan, and continue sautéing until they become tender.
5. Stir in the drained Tomatillos, Rotel tomatoes, Chicken broth, Chopped Green Chile Peppers, Oregano flakes, Coriander Seeds, Cumin, and Cannellini Beans.
6. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 20 minutes.
7. Return the cooked chicken to the pan, add beans, and stir gently.
8. Cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
9. Stir in Lime Juice and Black Pepper until well combined.
10. Serve hot in chili bowls topped with grated Cheddar cheese. Alternatively, place all ingredients (except cheese) in a crockpot and cook for 8 hours before serving. Enjoy with tortilla chips, sour cream, avocado dip, and Mexican beer.
